name: CVC
description: Control Voltage Converter for EaganMatrix instruments. One unit yields 16 high-resolution outputs for four voices, with i2c connectivity, MIDI pass-through and voice activity LEDs.

inputs.name: i2c IN (3.5 mm)
inputs.description: Primary i2c input for Slim Continuum, ContinuuMini, EaganMatrix Module and EaganMatrix Micro.

inputs.name: i2c IN (DIN5)
inputs.description: Alternate i2c connection for older Thick Continuums and Osmose-compatible use.

outputs.name: W1-4
outputs.description: Configurable outputs normally assigned to gate per voice.

outputs.name: X1-4
outputs.description: Configurable outputs normally assigned to pitch per voice.

outputs.name: Y1-4
outputs.description: Configurable outputs normally assigned to timbre per voice.

outputs.name: Z1-4
outputs.description: Configurable outputs normally assigned to pressure per voice.

outputs.name: MIDI THRU
outputs.description: DIN5 MIDI through connection for passing incoming MIDI data onward.

outputs.name: VOICE ACTIVITY LED
outputs.description: Per-voice status indicator showing when performance data is present.

behaviors.name: Four-voice mapping
behaviors.description: A single unit maps one EaganMatrix performance stream to four voices, each with four discrete outputs.

behaviors.name: Dual CVC expansion
behaviors.description: A paired setup expands to eight voices and 32 control voltages without changing the control protocol.

behaviors.name: Dynamic Base Voice
behaviors.description: The CVC can be configured so only selected outputs are active, letting one performance switch between multiple monophonic patches.

behaviors.name: Wide-range pitch bend
behaviors.description: The 16-bit outputs and 8-octave bend range preserve fast finger motion without the smoothing common in MIDI-CV converters.

behaviors.name: MIDI data playback
behaviors.trigger: MIDI data fed into the Continuum
behaviors.description: Performance data can be captured, played back and translated into control voltages through the CVC path.

behaviors.name: Voice LEDs
behaviors.description: Each voice LED flashes when data is active, which makes the routing visible at a glance.

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice translation
patchIdeas.patch: Connect a Continuum or EaganMatrix source to the CVC · route W for gate, X for pitch, Y for timbre and Z for pressure · patch the four outputs to four analog voices.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVC:
* Voice mode: 4 voices

Continuum:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
VCO 4:
Envelope:
VCA:

- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC (i2c IN)
- CVC (X1-4) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- CVC (X1-4) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- CVC (W1-4) g> Envelope (Trig)
- CVC (Z1-4) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Pressure-controlled filter bank
patchIdeas.patch: Use Z as pressure, X as pitch and Y as timbre · the synth voice tracks the finger while pressure fans out to filter cutoff and VCA depth for expressive dynamics.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVC:

Filter:
VCA:
VCO:

- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC (i2c IN)
- CVC (X1-4) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- CVC (Y1-4) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- CVC (Z1-4) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Eight-voice stack
patchIdeas.patch: Pair two CVCs with the interconnect kit · keep one voice group for a bass split and one for a four-voice pad · the same EaganMatrix performance fans out across the rack.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVC A:
CVC B:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
VCO 4:
VCO 5:
VCO 6:
VCO 7:
VCO 8:

- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC A (i2c IN)
- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC B (i2c IN)
- CVC A (X1-4) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- CVC A (X1-4) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- CVC B (X1-4) p> VCO 5 (V/Oct)
- CVC B (X1-4) p> VCO 6 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Voice split by base voice
patchIdeas.patch: Use the Dynamic Base Voice feature to switch between monophonic patches during a performance · one hand motion can reassign the same control stream from lead to bass to chord duty.
patchIdeas.diagram:
CVC:
* Base Voice: dynamic

VCO A:
VCO B:
Filter:
VCA:

- Continuum (i2c) >> CVC (i2c IN)
- CVC (X1-4) p> VCO A (V/Oct)
- CVC (X1-4) p> VCO B (V/Oct)
- CVC (Y1-4) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- CVC (Z1-4) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO A (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)
- VCO B (Out) -> Filter (In) -> Output (In)
